pain_point_label: Manual Marking & Weak Moderation Across Campuses

matches.match_rationale: AI can generate standardized answer keys, marking rubrics, and examiner guidance notes from curriculum specifications, directly eliminating the 'no standardized answer keys' problem. For essay questions, AI-generated calibration guides provide concrete anchor examples at each score band, giving markers objective reference points that reduce inter-rater variance across campuses.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A tool where exam coordinators input the question and syllabus objectives, and AI generates a comprehensive mark scheme with model answers, common error patterns, and partial-credit guidance — distributed to all markers before marking begins.

matches.match_rationale: Handwritten exam scripts must be digitized before any AI-assisted marking or moderation can occur. OCR and handwriting recognition converts physical answer scripts into machine-readable text, enabling downstream processing. This is the critical digitization step for education operators who still run paper-based exams across multiple campuses.
matches.opportunity_sketch: Scan or photograph answer scripts at each campus; OCR pipeline converts handwritten responses to structured text, feeding into a centralized marking and moderation workflow — eliminating the need to physically ship scripts between sites for moderation.

matches.match_rationale: AI can score short-answer and essay responses by classifying them against rubric-defined score bands, providing a first-pass mark that human markers then validate — directly addressing inconsistent human marking. Across multiple campuses, the AI provides a consistent baseline that flags divergence when a human marker's score differs significantly, enabling targeted moderation rather than full re-marking.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated first-pass marking system where AI assigns a score band to each student response; a human marker reviews and confirms or overrides, with the system flagging responses where human and AI scores diverge by more than one band for mandatory second-marking.

matches.match_rationale: Mark distributions across campuses can be compared statistically; AI identifies anomalous marking patterns — a campus where a specific teacher consistently marks 20% higher than the cross-site average, or where the distribution of scores is implausibly narrow. This directly addresses the 'admin staff manually reconciling marks' problem by automating the detection of inter-campus inconsistencies.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A moderation dashboard that ingests mark sheets from all campuses and flags statistical outliers — individual markers, question items, or whole cohorts — showing where moderation effort should be focused rather than requiring uniform re-marking of everything.

matches.match_rationale: The end-to-end moderation workflow — collecting marks from multiple campuses, running consistency checks, flagging discrepancies, routing scripts for second-marking, and producing final reconciled grade sheets — is a multi-step process that currently requires significant manual admin effort. An automation agent can orchestrate this pipeline across systems, replacing the manual reconciliation work.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A moderation workflow agent that automatically collects mark submissions from each campus system, runs cross-site statistical checks, generates a discrepancy report, routes flagged responses to designated moderators, and compiles the final approved grade sheet — reducing admin overhead from days to hours.
